387.reason: [Error: errorJ is called after match("LPARENT", ...) but before parsing the right parenthesis token. If the right parenthesis is missing, errorJ will incorrectly use the line number of the left parenthesis token (the last consumed token) instead of the line where the right parenthesis should have been. According to the problem description, the error line number for missing ')' should correspond to the line of the last non-terminal before the right parenthesis, which is the line of the left parenthesis or the line of the last token before the missing ')'. However, the current implementation uses the line number of the last consumed token (the left parenthesis), which may be incorrect if the missing ')' is on a different line. Additionally, the errorJ function does not consume the ')' token when it is present, leading to a mismatch in token consumption.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.0625 , reason:  [Error: errorJ is called unconditionally after match("LPARENT"), but the grammar requires a ')' to be present. If the ')' is missing, errorJ should be called, but if it is present, match("RPARENT") should be called first. The current code calls errorJ which attempts to match "RPARENT" and adds an error if not found, but it does not consume the token when it is present, leading to incorrect parsing and potential duplicate error reporting.]

This leads to incorrect parsing and potential errors in subsequent analysis.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [When a semicolon is missing, currentTokenIndex points to the next token after the expected semicolon position, but currentTokenIndex - 1 may not be the correct token that should precede the semicolon. reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [When SEMICN is missing, currentTokenIndex hasn't advanced past the last valid token, so currentTokenIndex - 1 may not point to the correct previous token. The error line should be based on the last successfully parsed token in the current syntax component, not currentTokenIndex - 1.]
